user_prompt: Use ubergoal to plan a risky multi-agent workflow refactor.
expected_behavior: create or bind a platform goal when available
classify the tier
run specialist review-board agents or lenses for Tier 2+ work
route detailed planning to uberplan
do not duplicate the full planning board inside ubergoal

user_prompt: Use ubergoal to decide whether this Tier 2 implementation is complete.
expected_behavior: create or bind the goal before treating completion as durable
use independent acceptance/review feedback rather than solo self-certification
route final proof to uberaccept
require evidence before completion
avoid calling update_goal complete before acceptance

user_prompt: Use ubergoal for a Tier 3 skill update and capture what the skill should learn.
expected_behavior: create or bind the platform goal when available
run Tier 2/3 specialist review-board agents when available
route planning to uberplan and acceptance to uberaccept
route post-run lessons to uberskillevolver
forbid silent self-modification

user_prompt: Use ubergoal for a one-line markdown typo fix.
expected_behavior: create or bind the smallest goal if the user explicitly invoked ubergoal and the runtime supports it
choose Tier 0
avoid subskills unless useful
avoid subagents

user_prompt: Use ubergoal to run an aggressive simplification and dead-code audit of this codebase.
expected_behavior: create or bind a platform goal when available
classify tier
run specialist simplification/review lenses before patching
route simplification trail and audit/plan/patch modes to ubersimplify
default to audit mode unless patching is explicitly authorized
still require uberaccept before accepting patches

user_prompt: Use ubergoal refactor campaign on this repo.
expected_behavior: load the reusable refactor-campaign profile instead of asking the user to paste a long checklist
create or bind a platform goal when available
run Tier 2 specialist review-board lanes for HOT files, KISS/modularity/dead-code, regex/structured-data, code review, and tests/evals
produce a tight generated HTML report and update project-local refactor history when artifacts are allowed
default to audit/plan unless patching is explicitly authorized
route simplification work to ubersimplify and final readiness to uberaccept

user_prompt: Use ubergoal nightly refactor campaign on this repo. We do this every few days, so keep the history useful.
expected_behavior: read the project-local refactor history before ranking HOT files
write or update a compact refactor-history.md when artifacts are allowed
include HOT files still hot/newly hot/cooled down plus no-change rationale
generate a concise HTML report that points back to history and evidence
avoid treating history as source authority over current code and tests

user_prompt: Use ubergoal to assess this X/GitHub/arXiv link before deciding whether to build it into Type0 or the skill pack.
expected_behavior: create or bind a platform goal when available
route source assessment through a specialist review lane before adoption
route source-to-recommendation due diligence to uberassess
preserve source authority and retrieval limitations
do not implement before approval
only hand approved build work to uberplan/ubergoal

user_prompt: Use ubergoal to finish this Tier 3 implementation. I especially want to know whether the implementation drifted into a fat harness and what tradeoffs or surprising choices were made.
expected_behavior: route final proof to uberaccept before completion
check implementation against the uberplan and OpenClaw/agentic architecture policy
verify thin-harness/fat-agent adherence and block deterministic monolith drift
use specialist review-board agents or lenses for Tier 2/3 final policy checks when available and allowed
report issues tradeoffs implementation choices and surprises in the final handoff
avoid solo self-certification or update_goal complete before acceptance

user_prompt: Use ubergoal to complete this work and tell me which Uber skills you actually used so I can verify we used our created skill system.
expected_behavior: include a Skills invoked summary in the ledger or final handoff
list ubergoal uberassess uberplan uberaccept ubersimplify uberskillevolver ubershow deep-rca and uber-skill-creator as invoked skipped consulted-only or not applicable
name where each used skill's artifact or evidence lives
explain why applicable skills were skipped instead of silently omitting them
do not claim completion with only generic planning language

user_prompt: Use ubergoal on a Tier 2 skill workflow and leave enough tracing for uberskillevolver to decide what should change later.
expected_behavior: create or update an Uber run receipt rather than relying on prose memory
record skills invoked skipped consulted-only and not applicable with artifact evidence
record plan acceptance uberaccept policy-adherence and skills-invoked gates
include fresh-agent replay mode or explicitly record the replay gap
handoff concrete receipt evidence to uberskillevolver
avoid building a hidden telemetry or orchestration monolith

user_prompt: Use ubergoal on this implementation. The same focused test suite has failed five times with the same clear error, so don't just keep pushing through.
expected_behavior: stop the normal patch/test loop at or before five consecutive clear failures
capture the failing command output and failure family in the goal ledger or receipt
route class-level diagnosis to deep-rca
revise the plan through uberplan before continuing implementation
append or merge any child-plan scope expansion, scope correction, or blocker into the parent plan
resume under the same ubergoal after the revised plan names the changed hypothesis and evidence gate

user_prompt: Use ubergoal on this implementation. A new integration test exposed an unexpected architecture problem that the current plan never covered, so don't just patch around it.
expected_behavior: stop the normal implementation loop immediately for the material unexpected failure
capture the failing command output and why it invalidates the current plan
route class-level diagnosis to deep-rca
create a focused child/sub-uberplan appendix when the RCA changes scope
append or merge the child plan into the parent uberplan as a scope expansion scope correction or blocker
resume under the same ubergoal only after the merged plan names the new hypothesis evidence gate and cost justification

user_prompt: Use ubergoal for this skill update. I want you to say upfront what you think I expect, what you might do that would surprise me, and what you'll ask or flag before proceeding.
expected_behavior: create or bind a goal when available for substantial work
perform a user expectation/surprise assessment before substantial planning
route the detailed assessment into uberplan for Tier 1+ or material work
record expectation/surprise risks in the ledger or receipt
verify expected-vs-actual user surprise before final completion through uberaccept
